titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of BL Add. Ch. 76747

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=674 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:45:10.219649+00 | translation moa=675 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:45:08.001957+00

p.cl: Omnibus Sancte Matris Ecclesie filiis . Tam presentibus quam futuris  Alanus filius Elti . Salutem .

Sciant omnes ad quorum noticiam
litere iste peruenirent  me concessisse et dedisse . et hac mea presenti carta confirmasse deo et Ecclesie Sancte
Marie de Melros . et monachis ibidem deo seruientibus pro anima patris mei et Matris mee . et pro animabus omnium
antecessorum et successorum meorum et pro salute anime mee et omnium heredum et parentum meorum  in puram et perpe
tuam elemosinam unam petram de cera ad procurandum lumen ante altare sancte Marie imperpetuum Hanc
autem petram cere Ego et heredes mei singulis annis ad pentecosten dabimus deo et domui de Melros 
scilicet nominatim de redditu illius terre . quam Howein filius Gillemihchel et heredes eius tenent de me et here
dibus meis tenore perpetuo in uilla de Thirlestan ... quicumque post ... ram tenuerint .

His Testibus .
Iocelino Glesguensi Episcopo . Simone Glesguensi Archidiacono . Aldred persona de Laweder . Rog’ lecras . Radulf lecras . Petro de la Hag . Thoma de Wa
rewic . Willelmo ... T pincerna . Willelmo filio pagani Henrico filio Baldewini
Willelmo clerico .
To all Holy Mother Church's sons, those present as well as those to come after, Alan son of Ælfsige [sends] greeting.

Let all those to whose notice these letters might come know me to have conceded and to have given, and by this my present charter to have made firm, to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose, and to the monks serving God in that same place, for the sake of my father's soul and my mother's, and for the sake of the souls of all my predecessors and successors, and for the salvation of my own soul and of all my heirs' and ancestors' [souls], in pure and everlasting alms one stone of wax to provide light in front of the altar of St Mary for ever. This stone of wax I and my heirs shall each year give at Pentecost to God and the house of Melrose, that is to say, namely in respect of the rent of that land which Owen son of Gillemichael and his heirs hold of me and my heirs in perpetual tenure in the toun of Thirlestane ... whosoever afterwards ... shall have held.

With these as witnesses: Jocelin, bishop of Glasgow; Simon, archdeacon of Glasgow; Aldred, parson of Lauder; Roger le Cras; Ralph le Cras; Peter de la Haig; Thomas of Warwick; William ... T. the butler; William fitz Payn; Henry fitz Baldwin; William the clerk.
